svg.title: Transmit signal chain - all band paths, with per-device working ranges
svg.desc: The full VHF/UHF transmit chain: a 7020-SDR exciter feeds the PE42512A SP12T band-select at low level, fanning out to all twelve ports. Each populated port is a band path - bandpass filter, CGH40010 driver, a power final, and a low-pass filter to a 50 W output. Every device box shows its technical working range: the BPF is cut slice-wide to pass the whole port; the CGH40010 driver works DC to 6 GHz; the MRF101AN final 1.8 to 250 MHz and the CGH40120F final DC to 1.5 GHz are broadband and cover each slice and beyond; microwave finals for 13/9/5 cm are TBD; the LPF passes from DC up to its cutoff. The BPF and LPF pass the whole band-select slice; the legal band-limit is enforced in software (the node allowed-TX table), not in the filters.
svg.text: Transmit signal chain – all band paths, with per-device working ranges

Each box shows the device’s technical range. The BPF is cut slice-wide and the LPF cutoff sits just above the slice top, so every box passes the whole port – the legal band-limit is set in software, not the filters.
spare – this 1.5:1 slice has no amateur allocation; leave unpopulated
spare – this 1.5:1 slice has no amateur allocation; leave unpopulated
low-level 50 Ω dummy / TX-inhibit park – cold-switch target
Reading the ranges. BPF = slice-wide cleanup passband. Driver CGH40010 = DC–6 GHz. Final MRF101AN = 1.8–250 MHz, CGH40120F = DC–1.5 GHz – both broadband.
LPF passes DC up to its cutoff fc (~1.1x the band top); it is a valid harmonic filter only in the ~1.5:1 window just below fc.

So for RF8 (2 m): BPF, driver, final and LPF all pass the entire 105–158 slice – the software allowed-TX table is what holds emission to the licensed segment (144–148, plus any authorized MARS/CAP adjacent to it).
The band-select slice, the slice-wide BPF and the LPF all pass the full port; the software allowed-TX table sets the legal band (one final still serves several bands).
Band-box colour = IARU region availability (R1 blue / all-region teal / R2 purple / varies grey). Ports loss-tiered (RF1/RF12 lowest loss). See docs/regions.md, filters.md.
